jokes.text: And at his brother's suggestion, he gave a false identity. Chareth Cutestory.
jokes.setup: Michael needs a false identity for a one-night stand
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'Chareth Cutestory' that sounds like a fantasy novel character
jokes.explanation: Perfect AD absurdist naming - sounds like a fake name but also weirdly plausible. Economy is perfect, and it's quotable.

jokes.text: Hey, you don't get giant checks if you're healthy.
jokes.setup: George Michael questions why Maeby is pretending to be sick
jokes.punchline: Maeby's cynical understanding of charity exploitation
jokes.explanation: Classic Maeby cynicism - dark but delivered matter-of-factly. Good character integration.

jokes.text: I figure I'll kill her off just before graduation. Just so everyone gets really sad before prom.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining her fake illness scheme
jokes.punchline: The calculated timing to maximize emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation of Maeby's scheme - the specific timing shows her manipulative intelligence.

jokes.text: Not a lot of kids have B.S. these days. - What does that stand for? I don't know. It's B.S.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks about Maeby's fake disease
jokes.punchline: The disease is literally 'B.S.' - bullshit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels - the disease name is literally what it is. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: The wife likes me in bright sweaters.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what Gob is wearing
jokes.punchline: Gob, the normally flashy magician, is being domesticated by his wife
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's unusual domestication, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: Just because I like it doesn't mean I have to wear it. I don't wear everything I like.
jokes.setup: Wife wants Gob to wear the sweater she bought
jokes.punchline: Gob's bizarre logic about liking vs. wearing things
jokes.explanation: Gob's weird logic is funny but not exceptional. Shows his character well.

jokes.text: 'Consummated.' What is up with that 'prosecutie'?
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Gob consummated his marriage
jokes.punchline: Gob calls the prosecutor a 'prosecutie' - mixing prosecute with cutie
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that sounds like something Gob would actually say. Good character integration.

jokes.text: What is with the insane moral code in this family?
jokes.setup: Michael won't open the evidence file on ethical grounds
jokes.punchline: Gob complaining about morals in the famously amoral Bluth family
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the Bluths are famously immoral, making Gob's complaint absurd.

jokes.text: Honey, they're just heels. They can only support so much weight.
jokes.setup: Lindsay falls while protesting in heels
jokes.punchline: Tobias's matter-of-fact observation about heel weight limits
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of an obvious truth. Tobias's clinical tone makes it funny.

jokes.text: Thou shalt protect thy father and honor no one above him unless it beith me... thy sweet Lord.
jokes.setup: Gob quotes the Ten Commandments to justify loyalty
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up commandment with fake Biblical language
jokes.explanation: Gob's invented commandment with 'beith' is perfectly absurd. Good Biblical parody.

jokes.text: I'm not sure that one made it down the mountain, Gob.
jokes.setup: Gob's fake Biblical commandment
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry reference to Moses dropping tablets
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Great economy and Biblical knowledge reference.

jokes.text: Ah, le clumsy adolescence. It's a phase we've all been through. Except for me. I was like a cat. I always ended up on all fours.
jokes.setup: Tobias philosophizes about clumsy adolescence
jokes.punchline: His 'cat-like' grace involves ending up on all fours
jokes.explanation: Tobias's pretentious observation undercut by the image of him on all fours. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Like a cat.
jokes.setup: Previous cat comparison
jokes.punchline: Tobias's satisfied repetition, oblivious to how it sounds
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Tobias pleased with his metaphor, unaware of implications.

jokes.text: I might even be the main idiot. Well, I'm not the main idiot, but I'm the idiot in charge.
jokes.setup: Michael confessing he's one of the 'idiot' Bluths
jokes.punchline: His correction that he's not THE main idiot, just the one in charge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael line - self-aware but still protective of his status. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I'm blind, Michael. I'm not stuffed up.
jokes.setup: Michael asks how she knew his identity
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that blindness doesn't affect her sense of smell
jokes.explanation: Good logical correction delivered deadpan. Shows character intelligence.

jokes.text: I smelled you, baby.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how she identified him
jokes.punchline: The casual, almost seductive delivery of 'I smelled you'
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of romantic tone with the mundane act of smelling someone.

jokes.text: Well, you lied to me. Chareth Cutestory?
jokes.setup: Michael's complaint about being set up
jokes.punchline: Maggie's incredulous repetition of the ridiculous fake name
jokes.explanation: Good callback that lets us hear how ridiculous the name sounds to someone else.

jokes.text: I know. 'Thou shalt not make up a name in vain.'
jokes.setup: Acknowledging his lie about his name
jokes.punchline: Fake commandment playing on 'taking the Lord's name in vain'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on actual commandment. Good callback to earlier religious references.

jokes.text: Well, I know Maggie Lizer is adorable. You don't know what you're missing.
jokes.setup: Maggie saying she hears Michael is cute
jokes.punchline: Michael's flirtatious response to someone who literally can't see
jokes.explanation: Ironic given she can't see, though the irony becomes deeper when we learn she's faking.

jokes.text: It's like we're making naughty sex even naughtier.
jokes.setup: Their forbidden relationship due to conflict of interest
jokes.punchline: The escalating 'naughtiness' of their situation
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about their situation escalating. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: 'Be true to thine own self, and to thine own self...' 'Be true.' Yeah. Number seven.
jokes.setup: Trying to quote the Ten Commandments about their situation
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong attribution of Shakespeare to the Ten Commandments
jokes.explanation: Funny confusion of Shakespeare's Hamlet with Biblical commandments. Good cultural reference mix-up.

jokes.text: I had a really interesting date last night. A woman who actually works two jobs.
jokes.setup: Barry being asked to review important legal documents
jokes.punchline: He's distracted by bragging about his date with a working-class woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - completely inappropriate priorities and classist amazement.

jokes.text: Uh, you. I was raising the children.
jokes.setup: Documents show evidence against 'us'
jokes.punchline: Lucille's immediate deflection and false claim about child-rearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - immediately shifting blame and making obviously false claims.

jokes.text: When was the last time you raised children? Excuse me? I've got two in the waiting room right this moment.
jokes.setup: Challenge about when she raised children
jokes.punchline: Lucille pointing to adult children currently waiting as proof
jokes.explanation: Good Lucille logic - technically true but completely misses the point of 'raising children.'

jokes.text: No. We have court day today. They take plea. Daddy come home. You out.
jokes.setup: Buster saying dad will be imprisoned long-term
jokes.punchline: Annyong's broken English threat that Buster will be kicked out
jokes.explanation: Good tension between the characters, with Annyong's broken English making the threat both cute and menacing.

jokes.text: I just winked. It's a surprise witness.
jokes.setup: Police officer telling the blind prosecutor about a surprise
jokes.punchline: Officer explaining his wink to someone who can't see
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd moment - explaining a visual gesture to a blind person. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: He feels you've been sucking off this family far too long.
jokes.setup: Gob telling Tobias what George Sr. thinks
jokes.punchline: The unfortunate phrasing that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic AD double entendre. Works on character level with Gob's obliviousness.

jokes.text: it's gonna be shape up or ship up.
jokes.setup: Gob delivering ultimatum to Tobias
jokes.punchline: Mangling the phrase 'ship out' into 'ship up'
jokes.explanation: Good Gob malapropism. Shows his character while being subtly funny.

jokes.text: This kind of agility? Let's go, little man.
jokes.setup: Gob mentioning the break-in needs agility
jokes.punchline: Tobias demonstrating 'agility' (presumably something awkward)
jokes.explanation: Callback to Tobias's earlier agility claims. Physical comedy we can infer from reaction.

jokes.text: Uh, I-I want immunity for me and my mom... and I want my dad to stay in prison. Also, can you send Annyong back to Korea?
jokes.setup: Michael pretending to be an informant with demands
jokes.punchline: The increasingly petty demands ending with deporting Annyong
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from serious demands to petty family grievances. Great structure.

jokes.text: He came back home once with a black statue... with an erect penis. It stayed in our living room until my aunt objected.
jokes.setup: Buster asked about father's unusual returns from trips
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of inappropriate art display
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster - innocent delivery of inappropriate content. Good family dysfunction.

jokes.text: This guy's wasting our time. Take away his grilled cheese.
jokes.setup: Buster not providing useful information
jokes.punchline: The petty punishment of taking away his grilled cheese
jokes.explanation: Absurd authoritarian punishment. Treating adult Buster like a child is perfect.

jokes.text: The cat is in.
jokes.setup: Tobias successfully entering Maggie's house
jokes.punchline: Reference to his earlier 'cat-like' agility claims
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with great economy. Tobias's pride in his 'prowess' is funny.

jokes.text: Justice cannot be blind, Doc. He's got a handle and everything. Come on, boy.
jokes.setup: Vet explains that Justice (the dog) is blind
jokes.punchline: Michael's confusion between the metaphor 'justice is blind' and his literal blind dog named Justice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay confusion between the legal metaphor and literal situation. The 'handle and everything' detail is perfect specificity.

jokes.text: I think she's been leading him around.
jokes.setup: Michael insisting the dog has been leading Maggie
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal - the 'blind' woman leading the actually blind dog
jokes.explanation: Great revelation that recontextualizes everything. Perfect setup for Maggie not being blind.

jokes.text: 'D.' 'B' places fourth. 'A'?
jokes.setup: Maggie taking LSATs while pretending to be blind
jokes.punchline: Her obviously cheating by seeing the answers
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing her cheating method. Good character moment showing her deception.

jokes.text: Don't worry. You're so ahead.
jokes.setup: Maggie in court while 'blind'
jokes.punchline: Her giving visual cues to someone while pretending not to see
jokes.explanation: Shows how she maintains her deception while actually seeing. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh! I can't see!
jokes.setup: Tobias caught in Maggie's house
jokes.punchline: His panicked claim of blindness (while wearing her perfume)
jokes.explanation: Perfect panic response that mirrors Maggie's fake condition. Great physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: Would that I could say that I wore it to impress you... but, no, I'm afraid it's merely a cloaking agent from a sadly blunderous afternoon.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on Tobias smelling wonderful
jokes.punchline: His verbose explanation of wearing perfume as camouflage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias verbosity and pretension. Lower economy score due to wordiness, but that's the character.

jokes.text: What's gotten into you? Have you been eating cheese?
jokes.setup: Buster acting uncharacteristically assertive
jokes.punchline: Lucille's bizarre explanation that cheese causes aggression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - absurd theory delivered with complete conviction. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Not just to me, but to all handicapped kids out there... who are sick and tired of sitting in the front rows at basketball games.
jokes.setup: Surely addressing crowd about inspiration
jokes.punchline: Complaining about the 'burden' of good seats at games
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - turning privilege (front row seats) into a complaint. Perfect Maeby cynicism.

jokes.text: Watch your head.
jokes.setup: Michael and George Michael walking near the falling Ten Commandments
jokes.punchline: Warning about physical danger right after philosophical discussion
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and irony. Great physical comedy timing with philosophical moment.

jokes.text: Surely, she has enough sight to catch the truth... before it hits her in the face.
jokes.setup: Michael's courtroom speech about justice being blind
jokes.punchline: Throwing something at Maggie to test if she can see
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and physical test. Well-structured courtroom scene.

jokes.text: That was my plan. What do you got?
jokes.setup: Michael's plan backfires when Maggie is actually blinded
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that his plan failed and asking for alternatives
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to failure. Michael's practical acceptance is funny.

jokes.text: No more B.S. No more B.S.
jokes.setup: George Michael saying he'll put an end to B.S.
jokes.punchline: Crowd chanting against 'B.S.' (both the disease and bullshit)
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the B.S. joke. Works on both literal and metaphorical levels.

jokes.text: I think I'm gonna go for... 'I was struck by the Bible and it restored my sight.'
jokes.setup: Michael asking if she'll admit to faking blindness
jokes.punchline: Her plan for an obviously fake miracle explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd solution that ties into the episode's religious themes. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, the hell with this then!
jokes.setup: Barry getting a supposed sign from God to lead a good life
jokes.punchline: His immediate abandonment of morality when inconvenienced
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - superficial religiosity abandoned instantly. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I was made to understand there were grilled cheese sandwiches here.
jokes.setup: New prosecutor replacing Maggie
jokes.punchline: His main concern being the grilled cheese mentioned earlier
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the grilled cheese punishment. Shows attention to detail.
